Founder's Note

Foundry Rooms began with a question: why are designers asked to imagine homes in showrooms that look nothing like homes?

The building at 700 N LaSalle is the long answer. It is a working architectural studio that has been adapted to also be a public gallery, an event venue, and a material library for the trade. Each program is independent. None of the operators report to me. But all of them benefit from being adjacent to the others.

What I hope for the building is that it functions less like a destination and more like a small institution: open often, free where free is right, careful about what it puts on view, willing to take time. The most useful design in a city is design that lets you see how it is made.

— Kuan Sun
Founder. Foundry Rooms. 700 N LaSalle, Chicago.

Programs

Four programs.
One address.

Independent operators sharing 700 N LaSalle. Visit any program; you are inside all of them.

Working Studio

— 01

Marfa Cabinet

Italian cabinetry, since 1970.

A working architectural studio specializing in kitchens, baths, and complete interiors. Designed in residence at 700 N LaSalle. Premium Italian cabinetry, stone surfaces, and integrated appliances. Five active commissions on view at any time.

Origin
Italy · 1970
Specialty
Kitchens, Baths, Interiors
For Homeowners →
Gallery

— 02

Hillary Contemporary

Contemporary gallery. Eight exhibitions a year.

A contemporary gallery operating across the studio's walls and gathering spaces. Solo and group exhibitions of painting, photography, sculpture, and works on paper. Curated by Hillary Contemporary. Free and open to the public.

Format
Solo · Group
Admission
Free

The Venue

Foundry Rooms.

— Three connected spaces. 2,400 sq ft.

Capacity
40 — 200
Format
Seated · Standing · Salon
Booking
Daily · Evening

Foundry Rooms occupies 2,400 sq ft across the building — three connected spaces that flow into one another, with wood parquet floors, gallery walls, and integrated AV and projection. The arrangement allows a single evening to begin as a reception, become a seated dinner, and end in conversation.

Available for design talks, product launches, dinners, salons, and private celebrations. Bookings begin at four hours and extend through a full day. Full event services available. By introduction for first-time hosts.

A room that has hosted a thousand conversations becomes part of every one that follows.

Plan an event at Foundry Rooms →
Exhibition 014
On View at Hillary Contemporary

Garden After
the Storm

— Arica Hilton

Run
Apr 12 — Jun 2
Format
Mixed Media
Admission
Free

Hilton's mixed-media paintings emerge across the studio's central wall — luminous, layered landscapes that hover between abstraction and atmosphere. Inspired by botanical resilience and post-storm light, the work converses with the surrounding cabinetry and materials in unexpected ways. The garden is what survives. The garden is what we make.

Visit Hillary Contemporary →
